title: How to train LearnGPT
summary: A copy-ready path from a clean clone to a validated FineWeb-Edu dataset, a complete 17.7M-parameter training run, and seeded text generation.

model.name: LearnGPT controlled 18M
model.dataset: Reproducible 1 GiB subset of the canonical 10 GiB FineWeb-Edu preparation

steps.title: Study the 42 checkpoints
steps.action: Run the numbered scripts against data/study_sample.txt while reading course_en.md.
steps.success: Every lesson runs without downloading the real training corpus, and each tensor shape has a clear role.

steps.title: Verify the environment
steps.action: Create the virtual environment, install the pinned dependencies, and verify CPU, MPS, or CUDA availability.
steps.success: PyTorch imports and the selected backend creates a tensor on the requested device.

steps.title: Run the repository gates
steps.action: Run the validator, regression tests, and the final self-contained lesson smoke test.
steps.success: Structure, English-only content, snapshot alignment, checkpoint logic, and the compact final pipeline all pass.

steps.title: Prepare the canonical corpus
steps.action: Stream and tokenize 10 GiB of FineWeb-Edu with GPT-2 BPE into train.bin, val.bin, and meta.json.
steps.success: meta.json reports complete=true, uint16 data, GPT-2 encoding, and byte counts that match both token files.

steps.title: Create the controlled subset
steps.action: Derive the seeded 1 GiB experiment without modifying the canonical 10 GiB source.
steps.success: The subset metadata records seed 1337, chunk size 65536, exact split sizes, and a complete result.

steps.title: Pass a backend smoke test
steps.action: Run a short fresh job with the final architecture and a disposable checkpoint name before committing hours to training.
steps.success: Initial loss is near ln(50257), gradients are finite, an evaluation is printed, and best/latest checkpoints load.

steps.title: Run the controlled training
steps.action: Start from random GPT-style initialization with a new checkpoint path and the documented 45,000-step profile.
steps.success: Validation loss trends downward, MPS gradients stay within the integrity limit, transient CUDA FP16 overflows retry the same step, and checkpoints are written atomically.

steps.title: Monitor the right signals
steps.action: Read training loss, validation loss, raw gradient norm, MPS retries, CUDA AMP retries and overflows, throughput, ETA, and target-aware context loss gain together.
steps.success: Retries normally remain zero, any CUDA overflow count stabilizes, loss improves over many evaluations, and correct contexts eventually outperform shuffled contexts.

steps.title: Resume safely when needed
steps.action: Resume from the latest checkpoint with the same data fingerprint and a total target of 45,000 steps.
steps.success: Model, optimizer, random state, schedule, mixed-precision scaler, dataset identity, and next step are restored.

steps.title: Evaluate the best checkpoint
steps.action: Generate several seeded continuations from the best validation checkpoint and compare prompt sensitivity.
steps.success: The model produces plausible English continuations that react to context, without being presented as an instruction-following assistant.

platforms.label: Apple Silicon
platforms.description: macOS on an M-series Mac. This is the profile used by the verified 45,000-step reference run.

platforms.label: Windows + NVIDIA
platforms.description: Windows PowerShell with an NVIDIA GPU and the pinned CUDA 12.6 PyTorch wheel.

steps.title: Clone and install
steps.description: Create an isolated Python 3.13 environment and install the dependencies for the selected backend.

mps.label: macOS Terminal
mps.code:
git clone https://github.com/ferdinandobons/learn-gpt.git
cd learn-gpt
python3 -m venv .venv
.venv/bin/python -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel
.venv/bin/python -m pip install -r final_project/requirements.txt

cuda.label: Windows PowerShell
cuda.code:
git clone https://github.com/ferdinandobons/learn-gpt.git
cd learn-gpt
py -3.13 -m venv .venv
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pip install torch==2.12.1 --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu126
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pip install -r final_project\requirements-common.txt

steps.check: The environment interpreter imports PyTorch without an error.

steps.title: Verify the backend and repository
steps.description: Confirm that PyTorch can create a tensor on the selected accelerator, then run the structural validator and regression tests.

mps.label: Verify MPS and project gates
mps.code:
.venv/bin/python -c "import torch; print(torch.__version__); print(torch.backends.mps.is_built(), torch.backends.mps.is_available()); print(torch.ones(1, device='mps'))"
.venv/bin/python -B tools/validate_learngpt.py
.venv/bin/python -B -m unittest discover -s tests -v

cuda.label: Verify CUDA and project gates
cuda.code:
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -c "import torch; print(torch.__version__); print(torch.version.cuda); print(torch.cuda.is_available()); print(torch.cuda.get_device_name(0) if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'CUDA unavailable')"
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -B tools\validate_learngpt.py
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -B -m unittest discover -s tests -v

steps.check: The backend is available and every repository test passes before any large download starts.

steps.title: Download and tokenize FineWeb-Edu
steps.description: Stream the dataset once and create the canonical 10 GiB GPT-2-tokenized source. Keep this directory unchanged.
steps.check: data/processed/fineweb_edu contains train.bin, val.bin, and meta.json with complete=true.

steps.title: Create and validate the 1 GiB experiment
steps.description: Derive the smaller deterministic experiment used by the controlled run, then validate the exact files training will read.
steps.check: Validation passes and the subset metadata records seed 1337, chunk size 65536, and a 1% validation split.

steps.title: Pass a short training gate
steps.description: Exercise the real backend and checkpoint path before committing hours to the complete run.

cuda.label: First 20 steps of the final CUDA run

steps.check: Loss is finite, the first loss is near 10.82, and best/latest checkpoint files are created.

steps.title: Start the complete training run
steps.description: Run the controlled 45,000-update profile. No artificial pause between steps is required.

cuda.label: Continue the validated CUDA run to step 45,000

steps.check: Validation loss trends downward, retries normally remain zero, and the latest checkpoint advances every evaluation interval.

steps.title: Generate from the best checkpoint
steps.description: Use the best-validation file, a fixed seed, and several samples to judge prompt sensitivity rather than one lucky output.

mps.label: Generate three seeded MPS samples
mps.code:
.venv/bin/python -B -m final_project.generate \
  --device mps \
  --checkpoint-path checkpoints/learngpt-mps-18m-stable-1g-v2.pt \
  --prompt "The purpose of education is" \
  --max-new-tokens 120 \
  --temperature 0.8 \
  --top-k 40 \
  --num-samples 3 \
  --seed 1337

cuda.label: Generate three seeded CUDA samples
cuda.code:
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -B -m final_project.generate `
  --device cuda `
  --checkpoint-path checkpoints\learngpt-cuda-18m-stable-1g.pt `
  --prompt "The purpose of education is" `
  --max-new-tokens 120 `
  --temperature 0.8 `
  --top-k 40 `
  --num-samples 3 `
  --seed 1337

steps.check: Continuations are plausible, react to different prompts, and are evaluated as base-model completions rather than chat answers.

parameters.name: --context-size
parameters.why: Number of tokens visible at once. Larger contexts cost substantially more memory.

parameters.name: --embedding-size
parameters.why: Width of every token representation; the main capacity and memory control.

parameters.name: --num-heads
parameters.why: Splits the 256-wide state into four attention views.

parameters.name: --num-transformer-blocks
parameters.why: Adds depth while keeping the model near 17.7M parameters.

parameters.name: --dropout
parameters.why: The controlled subset and run use no stochastic dropout, improving reproducibility.

parameters.name: --encoding-name
parameters.why: Selects the 50,257-token GPT-2 BPE vocabulary used by the dataset.

parameters.name: --batch-size
parameters.why: Micro-batches processed together; lower this first if the GPU runs out of memory.

parameters.name: --gradient-accumulation-steps
parameters.why: Combines eight micro-batches before AdamW, producing 8,192 tokens per update.

parameters.name: --training-steps
parameters.why: Total optimizer-update target, including completed steps after resume.

parameters.name: --seed
parameters.why: Makes initialization, batches, subset selection, and samples reproducible.

parameters.name: --base-learning-rate
parameters.why: Peak AdamW learning rate after warm-up.

parameters.name: --min-learning-rate
parameters.why: Cosine-decay floor used near the end of training.

parameters.name: --warmup-steps
parameters.why: Raises the rate gradually so random initial weights do not receive an abrupt full update.

parameters.name: --decay-steps
parameters.why: Spreads cosine decay across the entire controlled run.

parameters.name: --weight-decay
parameters.why: Regularizes matrix weights through AdamW without applying decay to biases and normalization scales.

parameters.name: --eval-interval
parameters.why: Measures train and validation loss every 250 updates without evaluating constantly.

parameters.name: --eval-batches
parameters.why: Averages enough batches to reduce noise while keeping evaluation affordable.

parameters.name: --gradient-clip
parameters.why: Limits the applied update after raw-gradient integrity checks pass.

parameters.name: --max-grad-norm-before-clip
parameters.why: Rejects a suspicious raw gradient instead of hiding it with clipping.

parameters.name: --gradient-retry-attempts
parameters.why: MPS can retry an invalid raw gradient; CUDA handles transient FP16 overflow separately.

parameters.name: --context-sensitivity-contexts
parameters.why: Checks whether correct context eventually beats shuffled context.

parameters.name: --output-chunk-size
parameters.why: MPS chunks the large vocabulary projection to preserve correct gradients; CUDA uses one projection.

parameters.name: --mixed-precision
parameters.why: CUDA uses FP16 autocast and a resumable GradScaler; the controlled MPS profile intentionally disables it.

healthSignals: The first loss is close to 10.82, the random baseline for a 50,257-token vocabulary.
MPS reports a successful repeated-gradient and CPU-parity self-check before step 1.
grad_norm is finite and grad_retries normally remains 0.
On CUDA, amp_retries normally remains 0; a small stable amp_overflows count means transient FP16 overflow was retried safely.
Validation loss improves as a trend even when individual evaluations fluctuate.
context_loss_gain becomes positive as the correct context becomes more useful than shuffled context.
Different prompts produce meaningfully different seeded continuations.
stopSignals: A non-finite loss or a non-AMP gradient appears.
The MPS startup parity check fails.
The raw gradient guard exhausts all retries.
CUDA exhausts eight same-step AMP retries or amp_overflows keeps growing persistently.
Validation loss persistently worsens while training loss keeps falling.
The operating system reports sustained thermal or memory pressure.
A resume command reports a dataset fingerprint mismatch.
